rulings.Case Title: MICHEL RANTISSI VS. FRANK SHATTUCK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Determine Prevailing Party On Contract And To Fix Amount Of Attorney Fees Allowable As Item Of Costs, Punitive Damamges,
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 9, 2013, line 1 - PLAINTIFF MICHEL RANTISSI's Motion To Determine Prevailing Party On Contract And To Fix Amount Of Attorney Fees Allowable As Item Of Costs, Punitive Damamges, OFF CALENDAR. Moving party failed to comply with SFLR 2.6B and this Court's order of September 12, 2013 to provide courtesy copies of the moving papers. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: FERMIN SOLIS ANIEL et al VS. T.D. SERVICE COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 9, 2013, line 2 - DEFENDANT AMERICAN HOME MORTGAGE SERVICING, INC., and DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED. TIF re Facts 8, 22, 41. Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Defendant failed to shift burden. See Fact 89. Yassen Declaration 25 fails to establish that Homeward was provided authority by DBNTC to modify loans. No purportedly reviewed documents attached to Yassen declaration to show that Homeward was loan servicer in September 2008; that DBNTC was the beneficiary; or that DBNTC gave authority. Additionally, Allonge of the Note is not dated and Assignment of Deed of Trust is dated February 22, 2010. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN MAYO et al VS. CADELINIA & LEUNG, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Reconsideration Of Court'S Order Sustaining Def Chicago Title Insurance Company'S Demurrer To Pltff'S Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, October 9, 2013, line 3 - PLAINTIFFs SUSAN MAYO, ELEXANDRIA DIETZ, ROBERT WEISS, PATRICIA WEISS, MEGAN FAULKNER, SIRR LESS, TIMOTHY DIETZ and JAMES DZIURA's Motion For Reconsideration Of Court'S Order Sustaining Defendant Chicago Title Insurance Company'S Demurrer To Plaintiff's Third Amended Complaint DENIED. Plaintiff failed to sustain burden under CCP 1008. Plaintiff's counsel states in her declaration that she was present at August 5, 2013 hearing and an oral argument took place. (Noll Declaration 3). No explanation is provided for failure to bring up the collateral estoppel argument at that time. Even if burden was sustained, Defendant is not estopped from demurring to the cause of action for Intentional Misrepresentation. "¿Bennett also affirmed the principle that when a plaintiff files an amended pleading in response to an order sustaining a prior demurrer to a cause of action with leave to amend, the amended cause of action is treated as a new pleading and a defendant is free to respond to it by demurrer on any ground." Berg & Berg Enterprises, LLC v. Boyle (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 1020, discussing Bennett v. Suncloud (1997) 56 Cal.App.4th 91. Additionally, September 5, 2012 order did not address the issue of reliance as Defendant's demurrer to cause of action for Intentional Misrepresentation did not raise this argument. (See Points and Authorities in Support of Demurrer to First Amended Complaint 3:25-5:8). = (501/REQ)

"¿Bennett also affirmed the principle that when a plaintiff files an amended pleading in response to an order sustaining a prior demurrer to a cause of action with leave to amend, the amended cause of action is treated as a new pleading and a defendant is free to respond to it by demurrer on any ground." Berg & Berg Enterprises, LLC v. Boyle (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 1020, discussing Bennett v. Suncloud (1997) 56 Cal.App.4th 91.
